title: 9 Herbal Essential Oils For Psoriasis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great natural remedy for psoriasis. Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, itchy patches on the skin. Herbal teas made from certain plants can help to relieve psoriasis symptoms. The key is to use herbs that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.  One example is Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold. It has been used for centuries to treat skin conditions, including psoriasis. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce redness and itching.  Urtica dioica, or nettle leaf, is another herb that may help to relieve psoriasis symptoms. The leaves of the nettle plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the skin.  Silybum marianum, or milk thistle, is a plant that has been used to treat a range of health conditions, including liver and skin problems. The seeds of the milk thistle plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce psoriasis symptoms.  Using herbal teas made from these plants can bring several benefits to your life. They can help to reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms, such as redness and itching. They can also promote healthy skin and reduce the risk of skin infections. Additionally, herbal teas can be a natural and non-invasive way to manage psoriasis symptoms, which can be a welcome relief for those who prefer not to use pharmaceutical treatments.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils contains triterpenoids, flavonoids, and carotenoids as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation. The anti-inflammatory effect of triterpenoids, particularly calendol and isoalantolactone, helps to decrease the swelling and inflammation associated with psoriasis. The antioxidant properties of flavonoids and carotenoids, such as quercetin and lycopene, help to neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from damage. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin health, Calendula officinalis essential oils may be beneficial in managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for psoriasis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula flowers and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to the glass jar. Stir well.
Add the dried Calendula flowers to the jar and close the lid. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the Calendula oil to a smaller glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3% of the Calendula oil with other carrier oils for psoriasis treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oils contains a high concentration of bioactive constituents like isoorientin and isovitexin, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis. The essential oil also contains ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Urtica dioica essential oils make it a potential remedy for soothing and calming psoriasis symptoms. Regular use of this oil may help reduce the severity of psoriasis and promote healthy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal essential oils for psoriasis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and stems. Wear protective gloves to avoid skin irritation.
Place the leaves and stems in a clean glass jar and add 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the Urtica dioica essential oil in a dark glass bottle, away from children and pets.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains flavonoids, particularly silymarin, and sesquiterpene lactones, including lactucopicrin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis. Silymarin has also been shown to have antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that can damage skin cells and exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. Lactucopicrin, a sesquiterpene lactone, has been found to inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, such as prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which contribute to psoriasis inflammation. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Silybum marianum essential oils may help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for psoriasis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum seeds and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass container.
Heat the carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water to 120°F (49°C).
Add 1 cup of Silybum marianum seeds to the heated oil and stir well. Let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the seeds.
Store the Silybum marianum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-annone, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce redness and swelling in psoriasis-affected skin. The oil also contains aloe-vera, which has soothing and moisturizing effects that can calm and hydrate the skin. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of aloe barbadensis essential oils help reduce the appearance of psoriasis symptoms, such as scaly patches and thickened skin. By reducing inflammation and hydrating the skin, aloe barbadensis essential oils can help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for psoriasis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis leaves and 1 cup of water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and put them in a blender with the water.
Blend the mixture for 2 minutes to release the oils and gel.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
Collect the liquid and transfer it to a dark glass bottle. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and beta-caryophyllene, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation and fight off infections that may contribute to psoriasis. The antiseptic and antifungal properties of the essential oil's active constituents, including terpinen-4-ol, may help soothe and calm irritated skin. The oil's ability to reduce redness and promote wound healing can also be beneficial for people with psoriasis. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for psoriasis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves, 1 cup of water, and a clean glass jar.
Wash the leaves with 1 cup of water and dry them with a clean cloth.
Place the dried leaves in a clean glass jar and pour 1 cup of water over them.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid and discard the leaves. Store the essential oil in a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two active constituents that help with psoriasis. Linalool has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis. Linalyl acetate, on the other hand, has antiseptic properties that can help prevent infections that can exacerbate psoriasis. The antifungal properties of these constituents can also reduce the growth of fungi that can contribute to the condition. By reducing inflammation and preventing infections, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for psoriasis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and clean them with water.
Place the clean flowers in a glass jar and cover them with 2 cups of water.
Steep the flowers for 2-3 hours in a cool, dark place.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container.
Store the extracted oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid and use it as needed for psoriasis treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and biflavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness and irritation associated with psoriasis. The essential oil also contains neryl acetate and geraniol, which have antiseptic and skin-regulating properties, promoting healthy skin cell growth. Additionally, the oil's high content of kaempferol and quercetin helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, common triggers of psoriasis symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth, Hypericum perforatum essential oils can provide relief from psoriasis symptoms and promote healthy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal essential oils for psoriasis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum (St. John's Wort) flowers and leaves.
Use a clean glass jar with a lid to combine 2 cups of carrier oil (such as jojoba oil or grapeseed oil) with the gathered Hypericum flowers and leaves.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil infuse.
After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the Hypericum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains bioactive constituents like alkylamides, flavonoids, and caffeic acid that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis, a condition characterized by thick, scaly skin patches. The flavonoids in Echinacea purpurea essential oils also possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the caffeic acid in this essential oil has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, which contribute to the development of psoriasis symptoms. By reducing inflammation and protecting the skin, Echinacea purpurea essential oils may be beneficial in managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for psoriasis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil.
Combine the Echinacea flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar, then stir well.
Place the jar in a sunny spot with indirect sunlight for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the Echinacea essential oil in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops per application for psoriasis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis. The antioxidant properties of aloe vera essential oils protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce the severity of symptoms. The acemannan in aloe vera essential oils also stimulates the body's natural repair processes, helping to promote healthy skin cell growth and reduce scaling. The combination of these properties makes aloe vera essential oils a potentially effective treatment for psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal essential oils for psoriasis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 10 drops of vitamin E oil.
Combine the aloe vera gel and coconut oil in a blender. Blend until smooth.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Add the vitamin E oil to the aloe vera mixture. Stir well.
Pour the aloe vera mixture into a dark glass bottle. Store in a cool, dark place.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease symptoms of psoriasis, but it's unlikely to prevent it from forming in the first place. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and peppermint, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe skin irritation. However, psoriasis is a complex condition that requires a comprehensive approach to manage.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for psoriasis can be a good option, but safety depends on the type and how often you drink them. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and peppermint, are generally considered safe. However, teas containing substances like aloe vera or turmeric may cause stomach issues or interact with medications if consumed daily.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can take a few weeks to a few months to show results in psoriasis. Some people may notice improvement in their skin after 2-3 weeks, while others may take 2-3 months to see noticeable changes. Consistency is key, so drinking herbal teas regularly can help manage symptoms.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for psoriasis can help soothe symptoms. Some people find it best to drink it in the morning, as it can help set a calm tone for the day. This might help reduce stress and inflammation that can trigger psoriasis flare-ups.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for psoriasis. Some herbs, like St. John's Wort, may affect how well the medication works or increase the risk of side effects. Other herbs, like ginkgo biloba, can thin the blood and make it harder for the body to heal skin lesions. This can affect the treatment's effectiveness.
faq_pregnant: It's best to be cautious when drinking herbal teas for psoriasis while pregnant. Some herbs can affect the baby's growth or cause other problems. For example, dandelion and sassafras teas may not be safe, while peppermint and chamomile teas are often considered okay in moderation. Always check the ingredients carefully before drinking.
faq_breastfeeding: If you have psoriasis and are breastfeeding, you can try herbal teas for relief. However, it's essential to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and peppermint, are considered safe for breastfeeding. But others, like goldenseal and evening primrose, might affect milk supply or baby's health. Always choose teas with proven safety for breastfeeding.
title_2: Treating Psoriasis in Women with 9 Herbal Essential Oils
